## 2026-06-16: GUI G1 ‚Äî differentiable RGB render (`render_hero_rgb_torch`)

The display-only `render_hero_rgb` severed autograd (it broadcasts Œ∏ with
`torch.full(..., float(val))`), so colour preference could not be steered through it.
Added `render_hero_rgb_torch(size, theta)` to `demos/gui/whole_frame.py`: a (H,W,3) colour
render that keeps the autograd graph through the compiled `hero_channel` substrate op for
ALL axes including the per-channel tints `cr/cg/cb` ‚Äî grad-preserving `val * ones`
broadcast (the same fix `render_hero_torch` uses), `torch.stack` instead of `np.stack`, no
`.detach()`. Each channel is the same composed mono hero tinted on the substrate (R‚Üêcr,
G‚Üêcg, B‚Üêcb), so geometry axes carry grad through all three channels and each tint through
only its own. TDD: `test_hero_rgb_differentiable.py` (5 tests) written first and confirmed
red, then green ‚Äî `grad_fn` set; ‚àÇ(red mean)/‚àÇcr ‚âÝ 0; green-channel loss leaks 0 gradient
into cr (channel independence); ‚àÇ(mean)/‚àÇbg == mean(cr,cg,cb) exactly; Adam through the RGB
render reduces loss. Measured 27/27 green locally (5 new + 22 existing hero/whole-frame),
no regression. Load-bearing prerequisite for G2 (RGB Adam controller).

## 2026-06-16: Rust frontend ‚Äî `..base` struct spread (functional update) (Phase 3)

Work-loop tick. Rust `Point { x: 9, ..base }` now lowers: extended the struct registry from
a name set to also carry per-struct field names (`_STRUCT_FIELDS`, captured from
`field_declaration_list` at registration), and `_struct_construction` now recognizes the
`base_field_initializer` (`..base`). The emit copies every declared field NOT given
explicitly from the base axon (`q.add("y", realvec(base.item("y")))`) ‚Äî Rust's
functional-update semantics on axons. New fixture `struct_spread` (`base={1,8}` ‚Üí
`q={9, 8 copied}` ‚Üí 9+8 = 17) compiles AND runs on the substrate to 17; suite 24‚Üí26, no
regressions. (This is the first frontend increment landing under the new transpilers-ci ‚Äî
CI will run it on push.)

## 2026-06-16: transpilers-ci.yml ‚Äî CI for all 9 sutra-from-* frontends (Emma greenlit)

Stood up `.github/workflows/transpilers-ci.yml` after Emma chose (AskUserQuestion) the
"full CI including F#/Erlang/Clojure" option ‚Äî the frontend suites had NO CI, so this whole
session's frontend work was substrate-verified locally but unprotected (and I'd just found a
real substrate bug in one). The workflow runs all 9 frontend suites (compile-AND-run on the
substrate) on push/PR to `sdk/sutra-from-**`: pip-installs the 6 wheel grammars
(rust/haskell/elixir/ocaml/scala/ts) + CPU torch + the in-repo compiler, and gcc-builds the
3 wheel-less grammars (F#/Erlang/Clojure) into `.so` on the Linux runner from their
tree-sitter sources (ionide / WhatsApp / sogaiu; parser.c [+ scanner.c]). Made the 3
loaders **platform-aware**: `_DLL` now picks `<lang>.dll` on win32, `<lang>.so` elsewhere
(verified Windows still loads the `.dll`). No ollama needed ‚Äî frontend fixtures lower to
codebook-free `.su`. Can't test the Linux grammar build locally (on Windows), so this needs
the live CI run watched + iterated (ABI mismatch is the main risk); verifying green next.

## 2026-06-16: F# frontend ‚Äî DU variants ‚Üí tagged axons (Phase 3) + substrate finding

Work-loop tick. F# discriminated unions now lower to tagged axons (the Haskell/Rust
shape). Added: `_union_info` prepass (`type T = A of int | B of int` ‚Üí `_VARIANTS[name] =
(tag, arity)`, tags per-DU in declaration order; DU type names ‚Üí `_RECORD_TYPES` so
`(s: T)` params type as `Axon`); `_variant_application` (a `Circle 4` application with a
variant head); let-bound DU construction ‚Üí `Axon c; c.add("_tag", 0); c.add("_val0", 4);`
prelude (reuses the record hoist); variant `match` patterns `| Circle r -> ‚Ä¶` test the tag
and bind the payload to `_val0‚Ä¶`. New fixture `union_axon` (`area (Circle 4) = 4*4*3 = 48`);
suite 22‚Üí24, no regressions.

**Substrate finding (planning/findings/2026-06-16-substrate-tag-equality-degenerate-at-
zero.md):** the FIRST version dispatched on an INLINE `(realvec(s.item("_tag")) == 0)` and
returned **32 = (48+16)/2** ‚Äî the 50/50 blend ‚Äî because substrate equality is degenerate at
the value **0** (a zero-valued filler read straight from an axon is a degenerate vector for
cosine equality). Measured the fix: binding the tag to an `int` local first
(`int _vtag0 = realvec(s.item("_tag")); (_vtag0 == 0)`) is crisp ‚Üí 48. This is exactly why
the working Haskell frontend already binds `_vtag` to a local. F# now does the same (emitted
to the function prelude). Scope to value 0 only; non-zero tags compared inline were fine.

## 2026-06-16: F# frontend ‚Äî records ‚Üí axons (Phase 3)

Work-loop tick, building on the let-sequence work. F# records now lower to named-field
axons (the OCaml/Rust/Elixir pattern). Added: `_record_type_names` prepass (registers
`type Point = { ‚Ä¶ }` ‚Üí `_RECORD_TYPES` so `_map_fsharp_type` types `(p: Point)` params as
`Axon`); a `_PRELUDE` construction-hoist mechanism (F# had none) ‚Äî a let-bound record
literal `{ x = a; y = b }` (`brace_expression`) emits `Axon q; q.add("x", a); q.add("y",
b);` statements before the function's `return` (the bare-path emitter now prepends the
prelude), with the var kept (NOT substituted); `_AXON_VARS` tracks axon-typed names
(record params + record-bound locals) so field access `p.x` (a 2-part `long_identifier`)
dispatches to `realvec(p.item("x"))`; `_record_fields` extracts the field initializers.
New fixture `record_axon` (`type Point={x;y}; sum2 (p:Point)=p.x+p.y; main = sum2 {x=5;y=8}`)
compiles AND runs on the substrate to 13; suite 20‚Üí22, no regressions (let_seq, typed_params,
match all still pass ‚Äî the long_identifier change only triggers for names in `_AXON_VARS`).
Discharges the dedicated "F# records/DUs ‚Üí axons" item; next F# step is DU variants ‚Üí tagged
axons.

## 2026-06-16: F# frontend ‚Äî let-SEQUENCE function bodies (Phase 3; unblocks records)

Work-loop tick. Extended `sdk/sutra-from-fsharp` to lower let-sequence bodies
(`let f x = let a = x+1 \n let b = a*2 \n a+b`). These parse as nested
`declaration_expression`s ‚Äî child[0] a `function_or_value_defn` value binding, child[1]
the continuation ‚Äî and the handler previously dropped the continuation (took only
child[0]). Now it does sequential `_SUBST` substitution (the Clojure/Haskell `let` shape):
bind `a ‚Üí (lowered e1)`, lower the continuation, pop; bindings see earlier ones (F# `let`
is sequential). New `_value_binding` helper extracts (name, value_node) from a
`value_declaration_left` binding (returns None for nested function defns ‚Äî a later item).
New fixture `let_seq` (`f 5 = (5+1)+((5+1)*2) = 18`) compiles AND runs on the substrate to
18; suite 18‚Üí20. This discharges prereq (b) for F# records/DUs ‚Üí axons (only the
construction hoist remains before records are straightforward).

the merge `cc3f868f` (last green `6a75e480`). Root cause: the merge brought
`demos/gui/test_gui_whole_frame.py::test_headline_banner_is_exactly_the_substrate_glyphs`,
which renders text via demos/font's `render_glyph` (`font_bound_antipodal.su`, 63
basis_vector calls ‚Üí codebook cached at d356, keys `font_bound_antipodal_p_*`). No
committed fixture covered those keys ‚Äî the gui fixtures had only d108 (letter keys), and
the font demo's d356/d484 hold the DIFFERENT non-antipodal `font_bound_p_*` keys ‚Äî so on CI
(no ollama) the test fell through to ollama and failed with ConnectionError. (I'd brushed
the "verify CI green, not just local" rail: individual gui test files passed locally because
my local ollama served the keys.) Fix: generated `nomic-embed-text-d356.pt` for the
antipodal keys (ran `render_headline_banner("SU")` with ollama + a fresh XDG_CACHE_HOME,
captured the cache) and committed it under `demos/gui/fixtures/`; the gui conftest globs
`nomic-embed-text-d*.pt` so it loads automatically. Verified CI-equivalent: full
`demos/gui` suite **47/47 passes with ollama UNREACHABLE** (`OLLAMA_HOST=127.0.0.1:1`).
Updated the conftest docstring (it wrongly claimed the gui demos use no basis_vector).

## 2026-06-16: Erlang frontend ‚Äî `#{K => V}` maps ‚Üí axons + `maps:get` (Phase 3)

Work-loop tick (non-GUI; GUI v7 awaiting review). Ported the Elixir/Clojure map-axon
machinery to `sdk/sutra-from-erlang` (previously MVP with no maps). Added `_map_key_name`
(integer/atom/string keys ‚Üí field name), `_map_fields` (`map_expr` ‚Üí `map_field` pairs),
`_maps_get` (recognizes a `remote` to module `maps` fun `get` with a static key),
`_hoist_maps` (hoist `#{‚Ä¶}` to a prelude `Axon _ahN; _ahN.add(...)` group, register
`node.id ‚Üí temp`), and `_maps_get_params` (type a param read via `maps:get` as `Axon`).
`_lower_expr` now emits a hoisted map's temp and lowers `maps:get(K, P)` ‚Üí
`realvec(P.item("K"))`. Wired into the bare-single function path (the common case);
maps in dispatch/recursion bodies remain a later item. New fixture `map_axon`
(`sum2(P) -> maps:get(1,P)+maps:get(2,P)`, `main() -> sum2(#{1 => 5, 2 => 8})`) compiles
AND runs on the substrate to 13; suite 12‚Üí14.

## 2026-06-16: Elixir frontend ‚Äî numeric arrow-map keys `%{1 => v}` ‚Üí axons (Phase 3)

Work-loop tick (non-GUI; GUI v7 awaiting review). Mirror of the Clojure numeric-key work:
generalized Elixir's `_string_key_field` ‚Üí `_static_key_field` to accept `integer` arrow
keys (the number's text becomes the axon field name), so `%{1 => 5, 2 => 8}` is the same
named-field axon as a string/atom map and `m[1]` (`access_call` with an integer key) reads

(`sum2(%{1 => 5, 2 => 8})`, `p[1] + p[2]`) compiles AND runs on the substrate to 13; suite
24‚Üí26.

## 2026-06-16: GUI rebuild R6 ‚Äî paper rewritten around the Adam demo; rebuild COMPLETE

Rewrote `paper/gui-steering/paper.md` around the Adam-through-differentiable-substrate
demo, finishing the GUI rebuild (R1‚ÄìR6). The central contribution flipped: the old paper
framed steering as host-side SPSA and ¬ß8 literally said "no gradients flow through the
substrate render" ‚Äî now FALSE. New framing: the render compiles to differentiable tensor
ops, so gradients flow through it, and steering is a gradient-based online-RLHF loop
(pairwise Bradley-Terry reward + Adam ascending R(render(Œ∏)) through the substrate).
Changes: title ("differentiable ‚Ä¶ gradient-based human-in-the-loop steering"); abstract
(Adam/RLHF + gradients-through-render, measured steering bright‚Üí1.0/dark‚Üí0.0); contribution
4 (gradient steering); ¬ß5 fully rewritten (SPSA‚ÜíAdam+online reward, SPSA noted as baseline);
¬ß7 rewritten (measured grad flow: bg ‚àÇ=‚àí1.0; directional results + the single-frame
instability we measured and why we use Bradley-Terry); ¬ß8 corrected (gradients DO pass
through the render; reward head + Adam still host-side); ¬ß9 (differentiable-rendering +
Bradley-Terry/RLHF threads); ¬ß10 repro (Adam demo commands); ¬ß11; +Bradley-Terry 1952 and
Adam (Kingma & Ba 2015) refs. Abstract 2269 chars. Gate on the transpiler track LIFTED;
clawRxiv loop RESUMED (this push triggers the v6 submission of the rewritten paper).

## 2026-06-16: GUI rebuild R4 ‚Äî live Adam window + run launcher

`demos/gui/adam_window.py`: tkinter live window on `HeroAdam`. Paints the CURRENT hero
(left) and a PROPOSED variant (right), both substrate-rendered; WARMER (W) = prefer the
variant, COLDER (K) = keep the current. Each press runs one Bradley-Terry reward step +
Adam policy steps through the substrate render, then draws the next pair so the hero
visibly morphs. `run_adam_gui.bat` launcher (`%~dp0`-relative so it runs from anywhere;
pause-on-exit) ‚Äî the `.bat` Emma expected and that didn't exist before. Window logic
smoke-verified headless (load module, build controller, propose‚Üíchoose‚Üípropose, _to_uint8
range 0‚Äì255, one WARMER press moved brightness 0.465‚Üí0.633); the tkinter mainloop needs a
display so it is untested in CI, same as the SPSA `steering_window.py`. Remaining GUI
rebuild item: R6 (rewrite the paper around the Adam demo), then resume the paused clawRxiv
loop.

## 2026-06-16: GUI rebuild R2+R3 ‚Äî Adam steering via online learned reward (RLHF)

`demos/gui/hero_adam.py`: the real-time-RL GUI demo Emma wanted ‚Äî Adam steering of the
substrate-rendered hero by a human warmer/colder signal, learning GRADIENT-based THROUGH
the differentiable Sutra render (R1), not the SPSA black box. R3 reward mechanism (Emma's
pick): online learned reward model. First built single-frame thumbs-up/down BCE; measured
it unstable and direction-inverting (a zero-init head + collapsing single-class labels
learned the wrong sign ‚Äî bright-preference drove the image dark). Switched to the
principled RLHF formulation ‚Äî **pairwise preferences (Bradley-Terry)**: each round proposes
current Œ∏ vs a perturbed variant, the rater prefers one, `loss = ‚àílog œÉ(R(better)‚àíR(worse))`
trains a tiny differentiable reward head (4√ó4 avg-pool ‚Üí linear), then Adam ascends
`R(render(Œ∏))` through the substrate render. Contrastive-by-construction ‚Üí stable in both
directions. Measured (size 16, 50 rounds, seeds 0‚Äì2): brighter-preferring rater
0.465‚Üí1.000, darker 0.465‚Üí0.000, steer flips with preference; 0 non-finite frames. Reward
+ optimizers are host-side (named so); the render is the substrate. Tests:
`test_hero_adam.py` 3/3 (both directions + flip, synthetic fixed-preference rater per
Emma) and `test_hero_differentiable.py` 3/3 (R1/R5: grad_fn, Œ∏.grad through substrate,
Adam reduces loss). `hero_spsa.py` kept as the SPSA baseline. Remaining: R4 live window +
launcher, R6 paper rewrite, then resume the (paused) clawRxiv loop.

## 2026-06-16: GUI rebuild R1 ‚Äî differentiable render path (Adam-ready)

R1 of the GUI demo rebuild. Added `whole_frame.render_hero_torch(size, theta)` + a shared
`hero_grid(size, dt, dev)` helper: the differentiable sibling of `render_hero`. It returns
the hero frame as a TORCH TENSOR that keeps its autograd graph (no `.detach()`), with Œ∏
entries as 0-d torch tensors broadcast grad-preservingly (`val * ones`, not
`torch.full(..., float(val))`). The numpy `render_hero` stays for display (the terminal
boundary). New substrate test `demos/gui/test_hero_differentiable.py` (3/3): the render is
a tensor with `grad_fn`; ‚àÇloss/‚àÇŒ∏ flows through the compiled Sutra `hero` op (bg grad ==
‚àí1.0 exactly, bright < 0, ‚â•3 axes nonzero); and 10 Adam steps through the render reduce a
brightness loss. This is the load-bearing fact for R2 (Adam can only steer Œ∏ via gradients,
and they pass through the substrate render). Gate: GUI rebuild before the transpiler track.

## 2026-06-16: GUI demo direction correction ‚Äî Adam + differentiable render (Emma)

Emma flagged that the GUI demo went the wrong direction. Intended: a product showcase ‚Äî
a pixel image generated entirely by Sutra, on which a person does real-time RL with
**Adam** to morph the GUI. Built instead: host-side **SPSA** (zeroth-order, black-box)
over a render that ends in `.detach().to("cpu").numpy()` ‚Äî every render path in
`whole_frame.py` discards the autograd graph, so the demo structurally CANNOT backprop.
That undersells Sutra's core point (the render is differentiable / every op trainable).
The SPSA was not invented ‚Äî it was ported from Emma's own `analysis/g1_simulation.py`
`spsa_dense` in the founder hub ‚Äî but Emma's current call is Adam.

Verified the rebuild is viable: passing Œ∏ as torch tensors with `requires_grad`, broadcast
grad-preserving (`scalar * ones`, not `torch.full(..., float(Œ∏))`), and NOT detaching, the
compiled Sutra `hero` op returns a torch tensor with `grad_fn`; `loss.backward()` gives
real ‚àÇloss/‚àÇŒ∏ through the substrate render (bright ‚àí0.24, bg ‚àí1.0, invs +0.76, accent
‚àí0.71 at neutral Œ∏; cx/cy/radius 0 by symmetry). The only blocker was `.detach()`.

Queue restructured per Emma (2026-06-16): GUI demo REBUILD (R1‚ÄìR6: differentiable render,
Adam core, warmer/colder‚Üídifferentiable reward, live window, substrate tests, paper
rewrite) is now the TOP item and a ‚õî GATE in front of the transpiler/ACTIVE-DIRECTIVE
track; the gui-steering clawRxiv loop is PAUSED until the rewrite (R6); a comprehensive
Sutra-documentation audit+rework is pinned at the very END of the queue (Sutra is a
business, not a research project). Work-loop cron repointed to the gated rebuild.
Structural facts confirmed for Emma: the GUI is a SEPARATE paper (`paper/gui-steering/`),
and `paper/paper.md` was never touched.

## 2026-06-16: Haskell frontend ‚Äî integer/float literal `case` patterns (Phase 3)

Work-loop tick (non-GUI; GUI was awaiting its v5 review). Extended
`sdk/sutra-from-haskell` `_lower_case_stmts` to handle literal patterns:
`case n of 0 -> a; 1 -> b; _ -> c` now dispatches the scrutinee DIRECTLY via an
equality defuzz-blend (`(n == 0)` ‚Ä¶) ‚Äî the Clojure/Elixir `case` shape ‚Äî instead of the
variant `_tag` path used for ADT matches. A `literal` pattern sets `test = (scrut ==
value)` and emits NO `_tag`/`_val` prelude (those are only meaningful for variant
scrutinees); `wildcard` `_` and a named-variable catch-all are bases (the named one binds
the scrutinee value, so `m -> m+1` lowers with `m = n`). Mixed variant+literal patterns
are refused (`UNSUPPORTED-CASE: mixed`) since a scrutinee is either an ADT or a number,
never both. New fixture `case_literal` (`classify 1 + classify 0 = 200 + 100`) compiles
AND runs on the substrate to 300; suite 18‚Üí20. Note before this: a literal pattern hit
`UNSUPPORTED-CASE: pattern literal`; `wildcard` `_` was also previously unsupported in a
case (only named `variable` catch-alls were) ‚Äî now both work.

## 2026-06-16: GUI paper v5 ‚Äî no-recompile property quantified (answers v4 con 3)

Work-loop GUI pass (top-priority item). v4 (post 2799) = Reject; the actionable con was
#3 "uniformity asserted, not measured." Measured it: new committed, reproducible
experiment `experiments/gui_norecompile_cost.py` times the one-time hero compile vs
per-frame render across many DISTINCT Œ∏. Result (64√ó64): compile-once ~3.6 s, then 200
distinct-Œ∏ renders at mean 1.3 ms/frame with **0 recompiles** (one compiled module id
across all 200 calls). Added a measured paragraph to ¬ß2 (where the no-recompile claim is
made) + the command to ¬ß10 Reproducibility. This is the concrete content of the ¬ß1
"uniformity" argument ‚Äî a per-call argument change, not a rebuild; host-side one-time
compile, substrate per-frame render. No throughput claim vs shaders (still explicitly
disclaimed). Loop continues (no bank).

## 2026-06-16: Elixir frontend ‚Äî string-key arrow-map `%{"k" => v}` ‚Üí axons (Phase 3)

Work-loop tick (non-GUI item, advanced while GUI v4 awaited its review). Extended
`sdk/sutra-from-elixir` to lower the string-key arrow-map form. (1) `_map_fields` now
handles the arrow form: `%{"k" => v}` parses as `map_content` ‚Üí `binary_operator`
children (`string` key `=>` value), distinct from the atom-key `keywords`/`pair`/`keyword`
shorthand; string keys give a static field name (`_string_key_field` reads the
`quoted_content`), so a string-keyed map lowers to the SAME named-field axon as the
atom-keyed one (`.add("k", v)`). (2) `_lower_expr` gained `access_call` (`m["k"]` ‚Üí
`realvec(m.item("k"))`, same projection as the atom-key `p.x` dot path). (3)
`_dot_accessed_params` now also types a param accessed via `m["k"]` as `Axon`. Non-string
arrow keys (numeric/interpolated/variable) still return None ‚Üí unsupported (need a
non-name field scheme ‚Äî left as the next Elixir increment). New fixture
`string_map_axon` (`sum2(%{"x" => 5, "y" => 8})`, `p["x"] + p["y"]`) compiles AND runs
on the substrate to 13.0; suite 22‚Üí24 (lower + substrate-run). Models on the OCaml/Rust
struct-as-axon pattern; mirrors the Clojure string-key-map increment.

## 2026-06-19 ‚Äî daily audit: clean (end-to-end, including semantic await tests)

73 .su compiled, 18 skipped, 0 user-program leaks + 0 runtime-prelude leaks (`experiments/substrate_leak_sweep.py`); `scripts/check_promise_await_fit_to_spec.py` returns `EXIT=0` with `[2/2] regression tests  PASS (4/4 expected)`; `tests/test_no_host_readout.py` 5/5 + `tests/test_substrate_leak_sweep.py` 1/1 PASS (6 total in 61s). Same shape as 06-15/06-16: after installing `pytest`, `numpy`, `torch` (CPU), the `ollama` Python pkg, and the Ollama daemon binary (`apt-get install zstd && bash /tmp/ollama_install.sh` from `https://ollama.com/install.sh`, then `ollama serve` + `ollama pull nomic-embed-text`), the 2 semantic-preservation tests (`test_await_semantics_preserved_{torch,numpy}`) DID run and PASS to 3 places against the fixture's expected 3.0; end-to-end promise/await verification is CLEAN, not partial. Compiled count unchanged 73 ‚Üí 73 since 06-16; the only commit touching `codegen_pytorch.py` since the 06-16 audit is `3c59427` (queue ¬ß0.3: compound &&/|| AND equality loop halts) ‚Äî adds `_VSA.truth_and/truth_or/truth_not` runtime methods + makes `_as_any_vector` scatter a 0-d tensor onto the real axis on-device (no `float()`) and `truth_axis` 0-d-safe; substrate-pure by the commit's own contract, verified by the 0-leak sweep. `await_value` body at `codegen_pytorch.py:935‚Äì966` is still the spec-compliant `return self.value(p)` reduction per Audit REAL LEAK #3 (FIXED 2026-05-17) ‚Äî no `for _ in range(100)` / `if self.isPending` signature anywhere; the prior body is referenced only in the leak-history docstring (lines 952‚Äì963, phrased without the literal old signature so the sweep doesn't false-positive). Audit.md REAL LEAK #1‚Äì#10 all still FIXED/NOT-A-LEAK at cited codegen sites: #9 (`eq`/`eq_synthetic` scatter) intact at `codegen_pytorch.py:2911` / `:2936` (0-d tensor scatter `out[self.semantic_dim + self.AXIS_TRUTH] = cos` / `= truth`, autograd preserved); #10 (`_select_softmax` scores) intact at `codegen_pytorch.py:74` (`_torch.stack([sc.to(...) for sc in scores])` grad-preserving stack with the raw-number `as_tensor` fallback at `:78`); #4 (`_TorchVSA.loop` fixed-T unroll) reconfirmed at `codegen_pytorch.py:3069` (`for _t in range(max_iters)` is structural-index unroll, body is `self._step(state, rotation, target, halted, k, threshold)` ‚Äî tensors in/out, soft halt via sigmoid, no `.item()`/`float()`/host branch on data). ## 2026-06-15: Clojure frontend ‚Äî `(get m :k)` access + string-key maps (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment, extending Clojure maps‚Üíaxons. (1) The function-call
accessor `(get m :k)` / `(get m "k")` now lowers the same as the `(:k m)` keyword accessor
(`realvec(m.item("k"))`) ‚Äî intercepted in the `list_lit` head dispatch before the generic
application path. (2) Map keys may now be **strings** (`{"x" 5}`) as well as keywords, both
yielding field name `x` (new `_map_key_name` helper, strips the quotes/colon). (3)
`_kwd_accessed_params` extended so a param read via `(get p :k)` also types as `Axon`.
Substrate-verified fixture `map_get` = 13 (`(+ (get p :x) (get p :y))`; `(sum2 {"x" 6 "y"
7})`); Clojure suite 24/24 compile-AND-run. Numeric/symbol keys + recursive-body maps remain
later items.

## 2026-06-15: Rust frontend ‚Äî nested/non-tail `match` in expression position (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment. A variant `match` may now appear nested in a larger
expression (e.g. `100 + match e { ‚Ä¶ }`), not only as the function tail. `_lower_match_stmts`
was parameterized with `tagv`/`valv` so a hoisted nested match gets collision-free binding
locals (`_vtag_hN`, `_val_hN_i`); `_hoist_enum_constructions` (the post-order construction
hoist) was extended to also hoist `match_expression` nodes ‚Äî emitting their binding
statements to the prelude and registering `node.id ‚Üí blend-expr` in `_ARG_HOIST`, so
`_lower_expr` substitutes the blend at the use site. The existing function-tail match path
is unchanged (default `tagv`/`valv` reproduce the prior `_vtag`/`_val{i}` output byte-for-byte;
verified by `enum_match` still passing). Substrate-verified fixture `nested_match` = 202
(`evalE e = 100 + match e { Lit n => n, Neg n => 0 - n }`; `evalE(Lit 7)` 107 + `evalE(Neg 5)`
95); Rust suite 24/24 compile-AND-run.

## 2026-06-15: Elixir frontend ‚Äî struct literals ‚Üí axons (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment, building on Elixir maps‚Üíaxons. A struct literal
`%Name{x: a, y: b}` parses as a `map` node with an extra `struct` child (the nominal
type) plus the usual `map_content` ‚Äî so it already flowed through `_map_fields`/the
maps‚Üíaxons path, lowering to the same named-field axon with the struct alias dropped (a
struct is structurally an axon, the Rust `struct`-as-named-field-axon shape). Made the
behavior intentional/documented in `_map_fields` and locked it in with a substrate-
verified fixture `struct_axon` = 13 (`sum2(%Point{x: 6, y: 7})`); Elixir suite 22/22
compile-AND-run. The `%{"k" => v}` arrow form and non-atom keys remain later items.

## 2026-06-15: F# frontend ‚Äî return-type annotations (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment. F# return-type-annotated definitions (`let f (a:
int) (b: int) : int = ‚Ä¶`) now lower with the annotated return type instead of falling
through unrecognized. These parse as a `value_declaration_left` (not the
`function_declaration_left` of the un-annotated form), and the curried-type grammar nests
params + the `: R` annotation differently per arity ‚Äî so `_extract_value_decl_left` walks
the subtree structurally: every `paren_pattern` (document order) is one `(x: T)` param,
and the return type is the `simple_type` inside a param-and-return `typed_pattern` (or a
direct `simple_type` child for a bare `let x : R = ‚Ä¶` value). `_lower_defn` refactored to a
unified path that threads the return type `R` into the emitted signature (was hardcoded
`int`). Two arity-dependent nesting bugs (params dropped for the 1- and 2-param forms)
caught by reading the lowered output before claiming done. Substrate-verified fixture
`return_type` = 13 (`let add (a: int) (b: int) : int = a + b`; `add 6 7`); F# suite 18/18
compile-AND-run. Return-type threading also exercised on `float` (`scale ‚Ä¶ : float`).

## 2026-06-15: Haskell frontend ‚Äî `data` ADTs ‚Üí tagged axons (Phase 3, variant tier)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment ‚Äî the full variant pattern for Haskell, ported from
the Rust enum machinery. A `data T = C1 a | C2 b | ‚Ä¶` prepass (`_register_data_types`)
registers each constructor as `_VARIANTS[Ci] = (T, tag, arity)` and `T` in `_DATATYPES`
(so `_map_type` maps it to `Axon`). Value construction `C arg‚Ä¶` (statement-shaped) hoists
to a prelude `Axon _ahN; _ahN.add("_tag", tag); _ahN.add("_val{i}", arg); ‚Ä¶` via the
`_ARG_HOIST` node-id mechanism (`_hoist_constructions`, which carefully skips
case-alternative PATTERNS and the head constructor of an `apply` ‚Äî two bugs caught by
reading the lowered output before claiming done). A `case scrut of (C x) -> r; ‚Ä¶` at the
function tail lowers to `_vtag`/`_val{i}` binding locals + a nested defuzz blend on `_vtag
== tag` with payload-name substitution (`_lower_case_stmts`, the Rust `_lower_match_stmts`
shape). New helpers `_register_data_types`/`_construction`/`_emit_construction`/
`_hoist_constructions`/`_lower_case_stmts` + `_VARIANTS`/`_DATATYPES`/`_ARG_HOIST`/
`_AH_COUNTER` reset per `lower()`. Non-variable scrutinees, nested payload patterns, and
`case` in non-tail position are explicit later items. Substrate-verified fixture `data_adt`
= 2 (`data Expr = Lit Int | Neg Int`; `evalE (Lit 7) + evalE (Neg 5)` = 7 + (‚àí5), matching
the OCaml/Rust `enum_match` ground truth); Haskell suite 18/18 compile-AND-run.

## 2026-06-15: Clojure frontend ‚Äî maps ‚Üí axons (Phase 3, data-structure tier)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment ‚Äî the data-structure tier for Clojure, mirroring
the Elixir maps‚Üíaxons shape committed the same day. A map literal `{:x a :y b}` (keyword
keys) hoists to a prelude temp `Axon _ahN; _ahN.add("x", a); ‚Ä¶` via the `_ARG_HOIST`
node-id mechanism (axon construction is statement-shaped, can't lower inline). Keyword
access `(:x p)` ‚Äî a keyword in list-head position ‚Äî lowers to `realvec(p.item("x"))`; a
param read via `(:k p)` types as `Axon` instead of the default `number`
(`_kwd_accessed_params`). New helpers `_kwd_name`/`_map_fields`/`_hoist_maps`/
`_kwd_accessed_params` + `_ARG_HOIST`/`_AH_COUNTER` reset per `lower()`. Non-keyword keys,
`(get m :k)` access, and maps in recursive bodies are explicit later items. Substrate-
verified fixture `map_axon` = 13 (`(defn sum2 [p] (+ (:x p) (:y p)))`; `(sum2 {:x 5 :y
8}))`); Clojure suite 22/22 compile-AND-run.

## 2026-06-15: Elixir frontend ‚Äî maps ‚Üí axons (Phase 3, data-structure tier)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment ‚Äî the first data-structure tier for Elixir. Ported
the Rust/OCaml arg-hoist machinery: a map literal `%{x: a, y: b}` (atom-key shorthand)
hoists to a prelude temp `Axon _ahN; _ahN.add("x", a); ‚Ä¶` with `_ARG_HOIST[node.id]`
carrying the temp name to where the map appeared (axon construction is statement-shaped,
can't lower inline). Field read `p.x` ‚Äî which parses as a zero-arg `call` wrapping a
`dot` ‚Äî lowers to `realvec(p.item("x"))` (the Rust struct-access shape); a param read via
dot-access types as `Axon` instead of the default `number` (`_dot_accessed_params`). New
helpers `_map_fields`/`_hoist_maps`/`_dot_accessed_params` + `_ARG_HOIST`/`_AH_COUNTER`
state reset per `lower()`. Scoped to the single bare-param `_lower_def` path; the `%{"k"
=> v}` arrow form, non-atom keys, and maps in multi-clause/recursive bodies are explicit
later items. Substrate-verified fixture `map_axon` = 13 (`def sum2(p), do: p.x + p.y`;
`sum2(%{x: 5, y: 8})`); Elixir suite 20/20 compile-AND-run.

## 2026-06-15: Rust frontend ‚Äî struct field-init shorthand (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment. Rust struct construction now accepts field-init
shorthand `S { x, y }` as sugar for `S { x: x, y: y }` ‚Äî `_struct_construction` handles
`shorthand_field_initializer` nodes (field name = the in-scope identifier, reused as the
value) alongside explicit `field_initializer`s. `..base` spread stays a later item.
Substrate-verified fixture `struct_shorthand` = 13 (`let x=5; let y=8; sum2(Point { x, y })`,
field-axon construction ‚Üí `realvec(p.item(...))` access); Rust suite 22/22 compile-AND-run.

## 2026-06-15: Clojure frontend ‚Äî `case` multi-constant test lists (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment. A Clojure `case` clause test may now be a
multi-constant LIST `(c1 c2 ‚Ä¶)` (a `list_lit` of number/bool literals), matching
when the scrutinee equals any member ‚Äî Clojure's list-test semantics, lowered to an
OR of `(E == ci)` tests folded into the existing nested-equality defuzz blend. Single
constants unchanged; non-literal list members surface as UNSUPPORTED. Substrate-
verified fixture `case_multilist` = 300 (`(case x (1 3 5) 100 (2 4) 200 999)`;
`(classify 3)` + `(classify 4)` = 100 + 200); Clojure suite 20/20 compile-AND-run.
Also trimmed two stale queue items: F# infra prereq (a) typed-param extraction (shipped
`1be5b175`); Clojure "multi-constant test lists later item" note.

## 2026-06-15: Phase 3 integration ‚Äî main Sutra paper gets a multi-frontend subsection

Phase 3 integration sub-task (part 2 of 2). Added a "Source-language frontends" subsection
to `paper/paper.md` ¬ß"The Sutra Compiler": the `.su` surface that enters stage 1 is itself a
compilation target reachable from nine source languages (OCaml/TS/Rust/Scala/Clojure/Elixir/
Erlang/F#/Haskell; C parked), each a pure tree-sitter source-to-source pass whose output runs
through the same five-stage pipeline ‚Äî so substrate purity is inherited from the one compiler,
not re-established per frontend. Documents the shared lowering-shape set (function / defuzz
blend / while_loop RNN cell / CPS trampoline / tagged+structural axons), the compile-AND-run
verification bar (OCaml reference 45 fixtures, others 6‚Äì19), and the unsupported-beats-mislowered
discipline. Measured/accurate, no overclaim. Triggers clawRxiv CI (papers-ci.yml) for the live
main paper. Completes the Phase 3 integration capstone (architecture docs + spec + main paper).

## 2026-06-15: Phase 3 integration ‚Äî frontends reflected in architecture docs + new spec doc

Phase 3 integration sub-task (part 1 of 2: architecture docs + spec; main paper next).
Brought the multi-frontend reality (now 9 active + C parked, Erlang shipped) into the
canonical agent-facing surfaces: AGENTS.md frontend row now lists all ten with accurate
2026-06-15 fixture counts (OCaml 45 ref, TS 19, Rust 10, Scala/Clojure/Elixir 9,
Fsharp/Haskell 8, Erlang 6, C 2 parked) and states the compile-AND-run bar + the shared
lowering shapes; CLAUDE.md cross-repo "Sutra owns" list expanded from `{ts,c}` to the full
`{ts,ocaml,rust,scala,clojure,elixir,erlang,fsharp,haskell,c}` set. New canonical spec doc
`planning/sutra-spec/transpiler-frontends.md` ‚Äî the cross-frontend contract: what "transpiles
to Sutra" means (source‚Üí`.su` pass, substrate-purity inherited from the one compiler), the
shared lowering-shape table (function/blend/while_loop/CPS-trampoline/axon), the strict-`<`
boundary caveat, per-frontend maturity, and the "unsupported beats faked" discipline. Grounded
strictly in tested code, not invented design.

## 2026-06-15: F# frontend ‚Äî parameter type annotations (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment. F# type-annotated parameters (`let add (a:
int) (b: int) = ‚Ä¶`) now lower to the mapped Sutra type instead of returning
`UNSUPPORTED-LET`. New `_FSHARP_TYPE_TO_SUTRA` map + `_map_fsharp_type` (the OCaml
`_map_type` primitive set: int/float/bool‚Üíbool, string‚ÜíString, unit‚Üívoid, plus
`double`‚Üífloat); `_lower_defn` extracts `(x: T)` from `typed_pattern` argument
patterns (`identifier_pattern` + `simple_type`) and carries per-param types into
the emitted signature. Untyped params still default to int. Substrate-verified
fixture `typed_params` = 17 (`let add (a: int) (b: int) = a + b; add 8 9`); F#
suite 16/16 (compile-AND-run). Return-type annotations (`let f (‚Ä¶) : T = ‚Ä¶`) use
a separate `value_declaration_left`/`paren_pattern` grammar path ‚Äî kept as a
later item; this also unblocks part of the records/DUs‚Üíaxons infrastructure.

## 2026-06-15: Rust frontend ‚Äî unbounded `loop { ‚Ä¶ break }` ‚Üí substrate `while_loop` (Phase 3)

Phase 3 transpiler-backlog increment. Rust's unbounded `loop { if COND { break; } BODY }`
now lowers to a substrate `while_loop` on the continue condition `!COND`. The single-leading-
break shape is exactly `while !COND { BODY }`, so it reuses the `_lower_while_rust` machinery
with the halt-guard hoisted out and negated via `_negate_cond` (a comparison inverts to its
negated operator ‚Äî `i >= n` ‚Üí `i < n`; otherwise `!(‚Ä¶)`). A `break` anywhere other than the
leading guard falls through to the unsupported path (`_contains_break` check). New helpers
`_loop_break_guard` / `_contains_break` / `_lower_loop_rust` in `sdk/sutra-from-rust/.../lower.py`;
`loop_expression` wired into `_try_lower_imperative`'s gate + dispatch. Substrate-verified
fixture `loop_break` = 15 (`loop { if i >= n { break; } acc = acc + i; i = i + 1 } acc`,
`sum_to(6)` = 0+1+‚Ä¶+5); full Rust suite 20/20 (compile-AND-run). Boundary caveat: the negated
break condition becomes the loop's strict comparison, so write `if i >= n { break; }` (negates
to strict `i < n`), not `if i > n`. README updated.

## 2026-06-15: FV ‚Äî optimization-equivalence verification (a real-world use of the arithmetic fragment)

Per Emma's "keep pushing FV substantively" ‚Äî the "bigger / real-world program" move,
building on the arithmetic-fragment expansion. Because arithmetic equivalence reduces to the
same polynomial-identity test, the checker verifies that a COMPILER OPTIMIZATION preserves
semantics. Measured (`test_verifies_arithmetic_optimization_equivalence`, exact ‚ü∫ randomized
agree): Horner's method `a¬∑x¬≥+b¬∑x¬≤+c¬∑x+d` ‚â° `((a¬∑x+b)¬∑x+c)¬∑x+d` (sound), identity folding
`(x+0)¬∑1 ‚â° x`, reassociation `2¬∑x+3¬∑x ‚â° 5¬∑x` ‚Äî all same-graph; a sign-bug "optimization"
`a¬∑x¬≤+b¬∑x+c` vs `a¬∑x¬≤‚àíb¬∑x+c` is caught as a different graph. The decision procedure is
unchanged; only the inputs differ. Directly answers the "demos too small / not real-world"
con ‚Äî semantics-preserving compiler-rewrite checking is exactly a trusted-base property, and
it is the same poly-time (Schwartz‚ÄìZippel) test. Added paper ¬ß3 paragraph, a finding
(`planning/findings/2026-06-15-arithmetic-fragment-and-optimization-equivalence.md`), test
(FV general 16/16). Triggers compiler-ci + fv-paper-ci.

`test_transcendentals.py` ccos/csin-vs-cmath (20 subtest failures) compiled `.su` using

2026-06-07 substrate-purity overhaul (they did `.item()`, severing autograd). Pre-existing

tensor, no host readout), the same extractors the passing exp/sin/cos tests already use.
Verified: `test_transcendentals.py` 8 passed, 46 subtests passed (compile + run + compare to
`cmath` within tolerance ‚Äî measured, not faked). Respects the no-readout rule (did NOT
re-add `.real()`/`.imag()`). Removed the queue RED item. Triggers compiler-ci (should now
be green).

## 2026-06-14: FV ‚Äî expand the verified fragment to integer arithmetic (Emma's keep-pushing call)

Per Emma's "keep pushing FV substantively" (and her "what philosophical objections?" probe ‚Äî
I'd overstated; "fragment too narrow" is a real do-more con, not philosophical). Found the
checker's decision procedure ALREADY decides integer-arithmetic polynomial equivalence
(`(a+b)*c == a*c+b*c` True; `a+b == a-b` False) ‚Äî the fragment is *polynomial expressions*,
not just AND/OR/NOT, and the paper undersold it. Made it a first-class, scalable capability:
- Extended the **randomized PIT** evaluator (`_eval_kleene_ast`, `_kleene_degree_bound`) to
  cover `+`/`-`/`*` and int literals + unary minus alongside the Kleene connectives, so the
  poly-time decision procedure handles arithmetic and MIXED Kleene+arithmetic identity at
  scale (was Kleene-only). Both exact and randomized routes agree on arithmetic
  distributivity/commutativity/square-expansion + a mixed case.
- Clean contrast result: arithmetic distributivity `(a+b)¬∑c = a¬∑c+b¬∑c` IS a same-graph
  identity (same polynomial), the exact mirror of Kleene distributivity, which is NOT ‚Äî the
  one checker decides both.
- Tests: `test_decision_procedure_covers_integer_arithmetic` (FV suite 15/15; broader FV
  23/23). Paper ¬ß3 broadened ("polynomial fragment ‚Äî Kleene logic AND integer arithmetic")
  + the distributivity-contrast sentence; abstract "Kleene-logic fragment" ‚Üí "polynomial
  fragment (Boolean logic and integer arithmetic)" (4991 ‚â§5000, trimmed a demos clause to
  fit); checker module docstring updated. Also stripped the leftover internal reference
  (`Audit's 2026-05-17 reclassification`) the bot flagged. Directly answers the recurring
  "limited to AND/OR/NOT" con with a real, faithful, measured expansion. Triggers
  compiler-ci + fv-paper-ci.

## 2026-06-14: FV ‚Äî worked two-gate composed circuit verified via the composition lemma (Emma's substantive move 2)

Emma's substantive move (2): verify a circuit larger than a single gadget via the
composition lemma. Added `and3_circuit_strict_min` to `fv-lean/Composition.lean` ‚Äî a
3-input AND (`z = (a‚àßb)‚àßc`) built from two AND gadgets wired on a shared spin `w`, whose
correct output is the **strict global energy minimum** for every input, discharged from
the two gadgets via the general `strict_global_min_of_terms` (NOT a monolithic re-proof).
`[propext, Quot.sound]`, no sorry; fv-lean suite 5/5.

Real subtlety surfaced and fixed (the known Ising chaining issue): a gadget's RAW energy is
not constant-0-at-correct (the AND gadget's min is e.g. ‚àí3, and varies with inputs), so
naive energy summation does NOT compose. The composing terms must be **proper penalties** ‚Äî
each raw energy shifted by its own strict minimum (`andE ‚àí andE_min`), 0 when satisfied and
>0 otherwise. With that, the composition lemma applies cleanly. (My first attempt assumed
min=0 and was wrong; the type-checker caught it ‚Äî an integrity win, no faked proof.)

Updated paper ¬ß7 (proper-penalty note + the two-gate worked instance), fv-lean/README, FV
spec. Abstract 4994 ‚â§5000. Triggers fv-lean-ci (proofs) + fv-paper-ci (‚Üí next review).

## 2026-06-14: FV ‚Äî general gadget-composition lemma machine-checked (Emma's substantive-work call)

Per Emma's blocker-sweep decision (substantive scope work, not more framing cycles), proved
the GENERAL gadget-composition lemma in Lean. `fv-lean/Composition.lean` (core Lean, no
mathlib; `[propext, Quot.sound]`, no `sorry`; full fv-lean suite 5/5):
`strict_global_min_of_terms` ‚Äî for any finite list of penalty terms over a shared state, if
`s‚ÇÄ` minimizes every term and every other state makes at least one term strictly larger at
`s‚ÇÄ`, then `s‚ÇÄ` is the STRICT global minimum of the sum. Proof via `sum_le`/`sum_lt`
(structural list recursion, integer steps by `omega`). Each gadget's `_strict` theorem
(AndGadget/XorGadget/FullAdder) supplies exactly these hypotheses, so a circuit of verified
gadgets has its correct output as the strict global energy minimum ‚Äî for ANY number of
gadgets, no monolithic re-proof. Includes a concrete two-term instantiation
(`two_term_circuit_strict_min`). This converts ¬ß7's composition *methodology* (an informal
argument last cycle) into a machine-checked theorem ‚Äî directly answering v70's "no
methodology for how the micro-proofs compose to a full program" con. Updated paper ¬ß7
(now "machine-checked in general"), fv-lean/README, FV spec. Abstract 4994 ‚â§5000. Pushes
run fv-lean-ci (proofs) + fv-paper-ci (‚Üí v72).

## 2026-06-14: Elixir frontend ‚Äî pipe operator `|>` (suite 18/18)

Added the pipe operator to `sdk/sutra-from-elixir`. `|>` parses as a `binary_operator`;
new `_lower_pipe` special-cases it before the `_OP_MAP` lookup: `x |> f(a, b)` inserts
the lowered left value as the right call's FIRST argument ‚Üí `f(x, a, b)` (`x |> f` ‚Üí
`f(x)`). Chains nest left-to-right via recursion on the left operand. Fixture
`pipe_chain` (`5 |> add(3) |> double()` ‚Üí `double(add(5, 3))`) compiles AND runs on the
substrate = **16.0**. Full Elixir suite 18/18. README + queue updated (pipe done; Erlang
noted as now its own shipped frontend).

## 2026-06-14: Erlang frontend ‚Äî new `sutra-from-erlang` transpiler (suite 12/12)

Implemented the Erlang ‚Üí Sutra frontend (Emma 2026-06-14 21:06 "implement erlang right
now"; Phase 1). No PyPI tree-sitter wheel ‚Üí `build_grammar.py` clones the WhatsApp
tree-sitter-erlang grammar and compiles `parser.c` + `scanner.c` into
`_grammar/erlang.dll` (MSVC, via a temp `.bat` to dodge cmd.exe's quote-stripping on
the spaced vcvars path), loaded by ctypes (the `sutra-from-clojure` pattern). Key
grammar finding: the WhatsApp grammar emits **one `fun_decl` per clause** (NOT grouped),
so the driver groups clauses by (name, arity) like the Elixir frontend. Lowering (modeled
on Elixir/OCaml): functions/calls/binary-ops; `if`/`case` ‚Üí defuzz blend; multi-clause
heads with literal/var patterns + `when` guards ‚Üí dispatch blend; single-clause `if`-based
tail recursion ‚Üí `while_loop`; foldable non-tail recursion ‚Üí CPS accumulator. All 6
fixtures compile AND run on the substrate: add_main=16, if_classify=100, tail_rec=15,
nontail_fact=120, guard_dispatch=150, case_dispatch=119 (suite 12/12). Multi-clause
recursion (Erlang's other idiom) + maps/records are documented later items. This is the
8th actively-developed source language (9th counting parked C).

## 2026-06-14: Haskell frontend ‚Äî `where` clauses + `let ‚Ä¶ in` (suite 16/16)

Added local-binding support to `sdk/sutra-from-haskell`. Both `where` and `let ‚Ä¶ in`
surface as a `local_binds` group of `bind`s (`variable` + `match`); new shared helper
`_apply_local_binds` lowers each binding's value (with earlier binds active) and
substitutes it for the name via `_SUBST` (the OCaml `let..in` / Clojure `let` shape;
numbers ‚Üí side-effect-free). `let_in` handled in `_lower_expr`; `where` applied around
the whole equation in `_lower_equation` (wrapped in try/finally so the binds are
cleaned up and never leak to sibling declarations). Fixtures (compile-AND-run on the
substrate): `where_block` = **31.0** (`f x = y + z where y = x+1; z = x*2`; `f 10` =
11+20), `let_block` = **18.0** (`g x = let a = x+1; b = a*2 in a + b`; `g 5` = 6+12,
verifying `b` sees the earlier `a`). Full Haskell suite 16/16. Mutually-recursive /
forward bindings are a documented later item. README + queue updated.

## 2026-06-14: Elixir frontend ‚Äî `when` guards on clause heads (suite 16/16)

Added `when`-guard support to `sdk/sutra-from-elixir`. A guarded head parses as a
`binary_operator` (`when`) with the `call`/`identifier` head as left operand and the
guard expr as right; new `_guard_split` detects it (operator text == "when"),
`_def_head` returns a 4-tuple `(name, params, body, guard)`, `_def_parts` rejects
guards (routes them through the clause dispatcher), `_lower_defs` threads the guard +
excludes guarded clauses from the bare-single fast path, and `_lower_def_clauses`
lowers each guard (under the same `_ai` param binds as the body) to a test ANDed with
the clause's pattern tests. Fixture `guard_clause` (`grade(n) when n>90 ‚Üí 100`,
`when n>50 ‚Üí 50`, `_n ‚Üí 0`) compiles AND RUNS on the substrate = **150.0**
(`grade(95)+grade(70)+grade(20)` = 100+50+0) ‚Äî compile-AND-run bar. Full Elixir suite
16/16. Last-clause-with-guard is treated as the base (test dropped), matching the
existing last-clause-is-base rule (documented). README + queue updated.

## 2026-06-14: FV mid-size mathlib step ‚Äî detailed balance + stationary uniqueness (Emma's blocker-sweep call)

Discharged Emma's "mid-size mathlib step" (her blocker-sweep answer to how far to push
the convergence proof): detailed balance + finite-chain stationary uniqueness, short of
full t‚Üí‚àû mixing. Set up an ISOLATED Lake project `fv-lean/mathlib/` (pinned mathlib
v4.30.0 = Lean v4.30.0; `.lake/` gitignored) so the core no-mathlib files keep their fast
CI path. `fv-lean/mathlib/GibbsMathlib.lean` machine-checks, over the reals (`lake build`
green, all `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no `sorry`):
- `stationary_of_detailedBalance` ‚Äî GENERAL: for any finite row-stochastic kernel,
  reversibility (detailed balance) w.r.t. œÄ ‚üπ œÄ is stationary.
- `gibbsKernel_detailedBalance` + `gibbsKernel_stationary` ‚Äî the clamped AND-decode Gibbs
  kernel with real Boltzmann weights `exp(-Œ≤E)` satisfies detailed balance w.r.t. the
  Gibbs measure, hence that measure is stationary.
- `stationary_unique_two_state` ‚Äî 2-state Perron-Frobenius stationary uniqueness (the case
  the clamped-decode chain inhabits): two stationary measures with equal mass coincide
  when `0 < P false‚Üítrue`, `0 ‚â§ P true‚Üífalse`.
With the core-only irreducibility + aperiodicity (GibbsChain.lean), this is the reversible-
chain convergence picture: positive + irreducible + reversible finite chain ‚üπ unique
stationary = Gibbs. Remaining (longer-horizon, BEYOND Emma's mid-size cap, not requested):
the t‚Üí‚àû mixing RATE / spectral gap.

Install/build notes (Windows): lakefile needs `git`+`rev` not a `version` range; `lake exe
cache get` hit a transient error-32 (Defender) rename failure that cleared on retry (8443
oleans, ~110s); first `import Mathlib` build ~10 min then warm rebuilds ~20s. Lean-API
fixes against the compiler: `Fintype.sum_bool` is `f true + f false` (made part-3 proofs
order-robust via `linear_combination`); `div_add_div_same` ‚Üí `‚Üê add_div`; detailed-balance
closes by `ring` (field handles `/Z`). Updated FV spec, fv-lean/README, queue (mid-size
done; mixing-rate residual longer-horizon). Paper ¬ß7 citation of this result lands as its
own commit/clawRxiv cycle.

## 2026-06-14: FV paper ¬ß7 reframed ‚Äî software FV vs HARDWARE FV (Emma's framing)

Emma's framing (mid-tick): "the first formal verification part is **software** formal
verification, and the second is **hardware** formal verification ‚Äî we could do this on
other hardware, but we're doing it on the hardware we're working on right now." This is
the real bridge the reviewer wanted (v64 called ¬ß7 "disconnected"): the paper's two FV
parts are two halves of ONE story, not main-result + bolt-on. Implemented exactly:
- ¬ß7 retitled "The energy-based compile target‚Ä¶" ‚Üí **"Hardware formal verification: the
  thermodynamic compile target."**
- ¬ß7 opening rewritten: ¬ß1‚Äì6 is *software* FV (verify the compiled program independent of
  its machine); ¬ß7 is *hardware* FV (verify the computation is correct on the physical
  substrate that executes it ‚Äî the answer is the configuration the hardware settles into,
  the ground state). The ¬ß3 reduction is what makes both halves one story; it is
  hardware-portable in principle ("the same energy-gadget obligations would certify any
  sampler minimizing the same energy") but instantiated on the specific thermodynamic
  hardware we're building toward ‚Äî correctness "at the level of the physics, not only the
  software."
- Abstract: "¬ß7 reports a second result‚Ä¶" ‚Üí "¬ß7 turns from software to **hardware** formal
  verification, on Sutra's energy-based thermodynamic compile target‚Ä¶" (4983 ‚â§5000).
- Conclusion ¬ß8: framed ¬ß1‚Äì6 as software FV and ¬ß7 as the complementary hardware-FV half,
  hardware-portable-but-instantiated, "the more consequential direction this work opens."
Honest (no overclaim). Supersedes the weaker "target-agnostic reduction" bridge from the
prior tick. Push triggers fv-paper-ci ‚Üí clawRxiv loop.

Also (separate, in progress): set up an ISOLATED Lake+mathlib project under fv-lean/mathlib/
for the mid-size convergence step (detailed balance + Perron-Frobenius stationary
uniqueness) ‚Äî Emma's blocker-sweep call. Not yet committed pending a verified `import
Mathlib` build (lakefile needs git+rev, not a `version` range ‚Äî that was the first error).

## 2026-06-14: FV paper ¬ß7 ‚Äî bridge the framework TO thrml (Emma blocker-sweep call)

paper.md` ¬ß7 so the energy-based (thrml) target reads as central, not bolted-on:

the thermodynamic backend"; (2) new opening bridge paragraph ‚Äî the ¬ß3 reduction (turn

## 2026-06-14: Clojure frontend ‚Äî `case` ‚Üí nested equality defuzz-blend (suite 18/18)

Added `(case E c1 r1 ‚Ä¶ [default])` lowering to `sdk/sutra-from-clojure` ‚Äî the `cond`
shape with an implicit `(= E ci)` test per clause, folded into a nested defuzz blend
with the trailing lone arg as the default (E lowered once + reused; numbers, so
side-effect-free). Literal number/bool test constants (multi-constant test lists
`(c1 c2)` deferred ‚Üí clean `UNSUPPORTED-EXPR`, never a mislowering). Fixture
`case_dispatch` (`(case x 1 10 2 20 3 30 99)`; `classify(2)=20` matched-clause +
`classify(7)=99` default) compiles AND RUNS on the substrate = **119.0** (the
compile-AND-run bar, not compile-only). Full Clojure suite 18/18. Reuses the exact
`==`-in-blend mechanism `if`/`cond`/`tail_rec` already verify. Updated the Clojure
README (supported constructs + Next) and the queue increment line.

## 2026-06-14: FV-in-Lean ‚Äî single-gadget Gibbs chain convergence floor (the "stochastic ODEs" item)

Discharged the BOUNDED sub-claim of Emma's "attempt a Lean convergence proof" item, in
Lean 4 core (no mathlib), `fv-lean/GibbsChain.lean` ‚Äî runner picks it up, full suite 4/4
passes (`scripts/check_fv_lean.sh`). Models the single-site (Glauber) block-Gibbs chain on
the AND gadget's `Bool^3` state space: a step changes at most one coordinate (`Step` = agree
on ‚â•2 of 3 coords; self-loops included). Machine-checked: `irreducible` (every state reaches
every state ‚Äî the 3-cube is connected; path flips differing coords one at a time) and
`aperiodic` (every state has a self-loop) ‚Äî **exactly the hypotheses** the classical
fundamental theorem of finite Markov chains needs for a unique stationary œÄ + convergence
from any start. Plus `strict_min_is_strict_mode` + `and_gibbs_unique_mode`: for any
strictly-antitone weight (every Œ≤>0 Boltzmann weight `exp(‚àíŒ≤E)` qualifies), the strict
energy-min is the strict unique MODE of the Gibbs measure ‚Äî so the chain converges (classical
thm, hypotheses now mechanised) to a œÄ peaked on the arithmetically-correct answer.
`irreducible`/`aperiodic`/`strict_min_is_strict_mode` depend on **no axioms**;
`and_gibbs_unique_mode` on `[propext, Quot.sound]`; no `sorry`. Honestly named as the
remaining mathlib step (NOT mechanised here): the t‚Üí‚àû limit theorem itself (Perron‚ÄìFrobenius
/ TV mixing) + detailed balance, which need real-valued probabilities + `exp`. Updated the FV
spec ¬ß "thrml compile-target" (the GibbsChain bullet + "still the mathlib step"), `fv-lean/
README.md`, the queue item (bounded part done; mathlib limit-theorem residual is longer-
horizon), and `paper/formal-verification/paper.md` ¬ß7 (new reachability paragraph; the "what
this does not yet prove" paragraph now says the ergodicity hypotheses + Gibbs mode are
checked and only the limit theorem is open; abstract clause "partly proven (ergodicity
checked, limit theorem open)" trimmed to keep the abstract ‚â§5000 chars; conclusion sentence
updated). The push triggers `fv-paper-ci.yml` ‚Üí clawRxiv loop.

‚Äî both with the headline overlay OFF and ON (the full RGB+glyph demo frame). The
per-frame substrate render survives a full session. **Directional consistency:** a
brighter-preferring rater drives `bright` 1.000 ‚Üí 1.800 (+0.800, the axis ceiling);
a darker-preferring rater drives it 1.000 ‚Üí 0.200 (‚àí0.800, the floor) ‚Äî the steer
direction flips with the preference. The running-best-brightness vs batch-index
Pearson correlation is ¬±0.446: moderate because the parameter saturates at the
clamp partway through then plateaus (it reaches the rewarded extreme rather than
ramping linearly) ‚Äî reported as measured, not massaged.

Tests (`demos/gui/test_hero_steering.py` 4‚Üí6; full demos/gui suite 39‚Üí41):
`test_soak_no_nan_blank_and_directional_both_signs` (100 presses, 0 NaN/0 blank,

`test_soak_full_demo_frame_stays_clean` (the RGB+headline frame, 0 NaN/0 blank).

optimizer + composition + warmer/colder bookkeeping are host-side; steering by a

## 2026-06-14: a1 item 1c ‚Äî warmer/colder steering controller + live window (gui-training)

Wired the warmer/colder loop. `demos/gui/hero_steering.py`: `HeroSteering`, a
HEADLESS controller tying `HeroSPSA` (host-side) to the substrate hero render.
Two-sided SPSA maps to two presses ‚Äî the controller shows the +perturbation
(first press scores r‚Çä), then the ‚àíperturbation (second press scores r‚Çã), then
runs one `update(r‚Çä, r‚Çã)` and begins the next batch; reward +1 warmer / ‚àí1 colder.
Each `frame()` guards against NaN/blank pixels (raises rather than painting
garbage). `render_hero_full()` (new, in `whole_frame.py`) is the full demo frame:
the Œ∏-driven RGB hero with the substrate glyph headline overlaid (banner placement
factored into a shared `_banner_placement` helper). Added an in-process compiled-
hero cache (`_hero_module`) so the soak's hundreds of renders don't recompile.
`steering_window.py`: a thin tkinter shell (WARMER/COLDER buttons + W/K keys) over
the controller ‚Äî I/O only, untested by design (no CI display).

Verified MEASURED (`test_hero_steering.py`, 4/4; full demos/gui suite 35‚Üí39): two
presses complete exactly one SPSA batch (counters advance 1 per 2 presses, phase
alternates); no NaN/blank frame across a 40-press session; a brightness-preference
rater steers the optimizer's best Œ∏ brightness UP from neutral by >0.3 ‚Äî end-to-end
directional morphing WITH the substrate render in the loop (not just the optimizer
in isolation); the full RGB+headline frame renders finite/non-blank with a preset
headline. Honest rails: the render is substrate (colour channels + glyph pixels);
the optimizer and warmer/colder bookkeeping are host-side; steering by a present
rater, not substrate-native training, not real-traffic learning. Next: 1d (full
100-press soak with the headline on ‚Üí the measured numbers paper ¬ß7/P7 are gated on).

## 2026-06-14: a1 item 1b ‚Äî host-side batched SPSA optimizer (gui-training)

Ported the SPSA optimizer that steers the hero. `demos/gui/hero_spsa.py`:
`HeroSPSA`, a host-side two-sided SPSA over the hero Œ∏, math verbatim from the
private hub's validated `spsa_dense` ‚Äî Rademacher perturbation, gains
`ck=c0/(j+1)^0.101` and `ak=a0/(j+1+10)^0.602`, `ghat=(r+ ‚àí r‚àí)/(2¬∑ck)¬∑delta`,
clamp to [-1,1]^D. Split into `propose()` (returns the two perturbed render-Œ∏
dicts to score) and `update(r_plus, r_minus)` (one SPSA step) for the interactive
warmer/colder flow. SPSA optimizes a normalized Œ∏‚àà[-1,1]^D (neutral start 0); each
continuous render axis maps from [-1,1] by an affine `center+half_range*norm`
(`HERO_SPSA_AXES`, e.g. invs‚àà[0.1,2.5], bright‚àà[0.2,1.8]) so SPSA stays in its box
while the renderer sees its ranges; headline weights pass straight through to the
argmax.

Verified MEASURED (4/4, host-only ‚Äî no substrate, so no torch needed): reward
`-||Œ∏‚àítarget||¬≤` ‚Üí final/start distance < 0.25 over 5 seeds (converges to the
maximizer); a monotonic-in-`bright` reward drives `bright` up ‚â•0.3 while a
signal-free axis stays near neutral (gradient SIGN correct); Œ∏ stays in the box
under a hard push and the batch counter advances 1/update; update-before-propose
raises. Honest rail: this is host-side SPSA over substrate-rendered output, NOT
substrate-native training ‚Äî the a1 spec's explicit framing. The proposed Œ∏ dicts
carry exactly the axes render_hero/render_hero_rgb/select_headline consume;
substrate wiring is item 1c. Next: 1c (warmer/colder buttons + live window).

## 2026-06-14: a1 item 1a COMPLETE ‚Äî colour channels for the Œ∏ hero (gui-training)

Added the colour axis, completing item 1a. `frame_hero.su` gains `hero_channel`:
the same composed hero (glow + ring + bg) tinted by a per-channel weight, in ONE
substrate op (the tint multiply is elementwise `hadamard` on the substrate ‚Äî no

times with Œ∏'s (cr, cg, cb) tints ‚Üí R, G, B as three whole-frame substrate fields,
then stacks them (display assembly, the frame_rgb precedent). Œ∏ gains cr/cg/cb
axes (default warm-white 1.0/0.85/0.6); HERO_THETA_AXES updated.

Verified MEASURED (whole-frame suite 15‚Üí16): each channel == host oracle (mono
hero √ó tint) to 1e-6, and Œ∏ drives colour ‚Äî a pure-red tint (cr=1, cg=cb=0) lights
R while zeroing G and B ‚Äî `test_hero_rgb_channels_match_tinted_oracle_and_drive_colour`.
Runtime-parameter (tints are broadcast buffers, no recompile). Honest rail: the
channel fields are substrate; only the 3-channel stack is host display assembly.

**Item 1a (Œ∏-parameterized hero render) is now COMPLETE**: render core + headline

Added the discrete "copy" axis to the warmer/colder hero: a headline chosen from

Œ∏-parameterized hero composed whole-frame in ONE substrate op. A single parameter

via elementwise `hadamard`/add; every Œ∏ component arrives as a per-pixel broadcast

## 2026-06-14: FV-in-Lean ‚Äî XOR + full-adder ground-state machine-checked

Extended the FV-in-Lean proofs to two more gadgets, same recipe (spins as Bool, energy to
Int, `omega` after case-split), both `[propext]`/`[propext, Quot.sound]` only ‚Äî no `sorry`.
`fv-lean/XorGadget.lean`: the 3-body XOR/parity gadget `E = œÉx œÉy œÉz` (negative weight) has
`z = x‚äïy` as its strict global minimum ‚Äî this PINS the correct sign, formally ruling out the
XNOR sign bug found+fixed earlier today. `fv-lean/FullAdder.lean`: the 1-bit full adder
(sum = a‚äïb‚äïcin via the 4-body parity factor, carry = MAJ(a,b,cin) via the pairwise factor)
has the correct (sum, carry) as the strict global minimum for all 8 inputs ‚Üí **addition's
ground-state decode is provably exact**, not just measured. The 2√ó2 multiplier is these
gates (AND + XOR) composed, so its correctness follows from the gate proofs. `scripts/
check_fv_lean.sh` now checks 3 files clean. Found a reusable tactic for the strict claims:
`first | exact absurd rfl h | (simp <;> omega)`. Remaining FV: the sampler-convergence
("stochastic ODEs") claim and the clawRxiv loop on the writeup.

## 2026-06-14: FV-in-Lean started ‚Äî AND-gadget ground-state machine-checked

First piece of the FV-in-Lean queue item (Emma: verify the thrml gadgets in Lean). Installed
Lean 4.30.0 via elan (core only, no mathlib). `fv-lean/AndGadget.lean` PROVES what approach
A2 only measured (100%) and approach C re-learned: the derived AND Ising gadget has the
arithmetically-correct output `z = a AND b` as its STRICT global energy minimum ‚Äî
`and_gadget_min` (correct output attains the minimum for every input) + `and_gadget_strict`
(every wrong output strictly higher ‚Üí unique minimiser). Both depend only on
`[propext, Quot.sound]`, NO `sorry` (`#print axioms` checked). Recipe: spins as Bool, energy
√ó4 to Int, 8 configs closed by `omega` after case-splitting (`decide` gets stuck on Int in
the kernel ‚Äî used and rejected). `scripts/check_fv_lean.sh` runs the proofs and fails on any
error/sorry. Lean is NOT in CI yet (toolchain install is heavy) ‚Äî local check for now.
Remaining FV work: XOR/parity + full-adder + multiplier ground-state proofs (same recipe),
the sampler-convergence ("stochastic ODEs") claim, and the clawRxiv loop on the writeup.

## 2026-06-14: Sutra ‚Üí thrml ‚Äî hardware-alignment notes; the A‚ÄìH track is COMPLETE

Wrote `planning/findings/2026-06-14-thrml-hardware-alignment.md`, grounded in the Extropic
TSU architecture paper vendored in the thrml submodule. The chip is a sparse,
locally-connected, **pairwise** grid of p-bits that samples via two-color block Gibbs; the
**host programs the weights and reads the result** ‚Äî which matches exactly the host/sampled
split our approaches use (chip samples; host does weight-setup, verifier, decode,
compositor). Non-obvious finding: the **AND gadget and the adder carry are already
pairwise + biases ‚Üí TSU-native**, while bind/XOR/parity (3-/4-body factors) need a local
auxiliary-spin reduction, and the dense all-to-all Hopfield memory is the LEAST
chip-aligned (a prototyping-layer strength, not the chip's). This refines the H
recommendation for real deployment: keep the emitted graph sparse + 2-body, prefer the
gate-circuit + sample-and-verify path. Deferred (named): a 3-/4-body ‚Üí 2-body
auxiliary-spin reduction pass in codegen_thrml.

With this, the whole **Sutra ‚Üí thrml track is complete** ‚Äî six validated approaches (A‚ÄìF),
a non-destructive compiler backend (G, `--emit-thrml`, compiles AND samples), the
head-to-head comparison + standardization call (H), and hardware-alignment notes. From a
cold start it established, measured on real thrml, that Sutra computation ‚Äî memory,
bind/unbind, arithmetic, composed programs, and a `.su`‚Üíthrml compile path ‚Äî runs on
Extropic's thermodynamic sampler. The queue now rolls into the FV-in-Lean item (formally
proving the ground-state claims) and the a1 demo.

## 2026-06-14: Sutra ‚Üí thrml ‚Äî approach H (compare all) done; recommendation set

Wrote the head-to-head comparison of the A‚ÄìG approaches (Emma's deliverable):
`planning/findings/2026-06-14-thrml-approaches-comparison.md` ‚Äî a measured table
(every number from the attempt log, on real thrml), the cross-cutting trade-offs
(verifier vs ground-state vs learned decode; cold-vs-warm Œ≤; staged-vs-joint
composition; bit-register-vs-categorical encoding; hand-built-vs-learned couplings),
and a concrete "what to standardize on": **bit-registers + sample-and-verify (A) as
the default lowering** (general, robust, what codegen G targets), ground-state
decode (B) as the verifier-free fast path for sign-correct gadgets, categorical (D)
for small-domain maps, structured codes (F) + trainable couplings (C) for
associative-memory capacity, staged composition by default with joint-EBM (E)
reserved for readout-purity. So the whole thrml track A‚ÄìH is complete: six
validated approaches, a working non-destructive codegen, and a standardization
call. Remaining thrml item: hardware-alignment notes; then the queue rolls into the
FV-in-Lean item (formally proving the B-style ground-state claims this doc measured)
and the a1 demo.

## 2026-06-14: Sutra ‚Üí thrml codegen G.3 ‚Äî bind/unbind op-graphs (round-trip 1.000)

Generalized `codegen_thrml.translate_thrml` from one-op bind to a small bind/unbind
OP-GRAPH: a `main` body of `vector tmp = bind/unbind(x, y);` intermediates plus a final
`return ‚Ä¶` over `basis_vector` atoms. Each value is a 16-bit spin register; every
bind/unbind is the 3-body product factor `in1_i¬∑in2_i¬∑out_i=+1`; atoms are clamped and
all intermediates+result are sampled jointly; the emitted program self-verifies against
the host-computed element-wise-product ground truth. New `examples/thrml_roundtrip.su`
(the canonical VSA identity `unbind(bind(a,b),a)=b`, a 2-factor program): compiles AND
samples = **1.000** per-bit; single-op `thrml_bind.su` still 1.000. `test_codegen_thrml.py`
now 4/4 (compile-AND-sample parametrized over both fixtures + the two non-destructive
guards). Daily 2026-06-14 substrate-honesty audit reviewed clean and discharged. Approach
G is done through op-graphs; `bundle`/`unbind`+cleanup (needs a codebook decode) and
`==`/AND gadgets are deferred, non-blocking for H (compare all).

## 2026-06-14: Sutra ‚Üí thrml codegen G.1‚ÄìG.2 ‚Äî `bind` compiles AND samples (1.000)

The thrml backend now lowers a real Sutra program end-to-end. `codegen_thrml.translate_thrml`
walks the AST: `main(){ return bind/unbind(a, b); }` over top-level `basis_vector` atoms
lowers to a self-verifying thrml/JAX program ‚Äî each atom a deterministic 16-bit spin
register, `bind` the 3-body product factor `a_i¬∑b_i¬∑u_i=+1`, the result by block-Gibbs
sampling. `examples/thrml_bind.su`: emitted program RUNS and the sampled `u` matches
ground-truth `a‚äôb` per-bit = **1.000**. New `tests/test_codegen_thrml.py` (3/3): the
compile-AND-sample check (skips without jax/thrml) plus two non-destructive guards ‚Äî an
unsupported construct gives a clean `thrml-codegen:` diagnostic (exit 2, no traceback, no
mislowering), and the PyTorch `--run` path still compiles the same `.su`. So one `.su`
now compiles on BOTH backends. Anything outside the one-op-bind subset raises
`ThrmlCodegenNotSupported` with a precise reason. Next: G.3 broaden ops (bundle, unbind+
cleanup, multi-statement bodies), then H (compare all).

## 2026-06-13: Sutra ‚Üí thrml ‚Äî exploration loop attempts #1‚Äì#3 (memory + bind both WORK)

Emma reframed the thrml track as an exploration loop: try approaches until Sutra
computation actually runs on Extropic's sampler, measuring each. Three attempts so far,
all RUN + measured on the thrml substrate (JAX-CPU), all in `experiments/thrml/`:

- **#1 associative memory** ‚Äî value = N-bit spin register; bundle = Hebbian couplings;
  cleanup = block-Gibbs. Œ≤=6 ‚Üí 96.8% of samples recover a stored value vs 0% baseline.
- **#2 clamped-cue retrieval** ‚Äî clamp half a value's bits, infer the rest. Œ≤=6 ‚Üí 99.2%
  per-bit vs 50% baseline. Honest capacity wall measured: clean to ~Hopfield 0.14¬∑N
  (M‚â§3 for N=16), crosstalk beyond (M=4 ‚Üí 84%).
- **#3 bind/unbind** ‚Äî the transformational op. Answer to "energy or different trick?":
  an **energy** ‚Äî bipolar bind `c_i=a_i b_i` is the 3-body constraint `a_i b_i c_i=+1`,
  expressible via thrml's arbitrary-arity `SpinEBMFactor` (not pairwise Ising). Œ≤=6 ‚Üí
  bind 1.000, unbind 1.000 per-bit vs 0.5. No capacity wall (per-bit independent).

So both Sutra operation classes ‚Äî memory (bundle/cleanup/retrieval) and transformational
(bind/unbind) ‚Äî genuinely compute on the thermodynamic sampler, measured. Attempt log:
`planning/open-questions/2026-06-13-sutra-to-thrml-mapping.md`. Non-destructive: all
additive (submodule + isolated JAX dep + experiments/), the PyTorch pipeline untouched.
Next: arithmetic-as-energy, a composed pipeline, then `codegen_thrml` behind an additive
`--target thrml` flag.
